title: auth-service login failures after partner credential expiry
error_signature: 401 Unauthorized from partner-api (token expired) -> 403 login denied
summary: Starting exactly at 00:00 UTC, auth-service stopped minting sessions: calls to partner-api (used for identity verification) return 401, cascading into 403 login failures for all users. No deploy occurred and traffic is flat.

ground_truth.root_cause: The API credential auth-service uses to call partner-api expired at 2026-06-19T00:00:00Z. partner-api now rejects every request with 401 ('token expired'), so auth-service cannot complete identity verification and denies all logins with 403. The midnight-sharp onset, absence of any deploy, and flat traffic point to a credential/cert lifecycle expiry, not a code or load problem.
key_evidence: partner-api log: '401 Unauthorized: API token expired at 2026-06-19T00:00:00Z (kid=key-2025-06)'
auth-service log: 'identity verification failed: partner-api returned 401' then '403 login denied'
auth-service log: 'no deploys in last 24h; traffic nominal' - rules out a bad deploy or load spike
expected_fix_themes: rotate/renew the expired partner-api credential (and signing cert if applicable) to restore logins
add expiry monitoring and alerts ahead of credential/cert expiration
automate credential rotation and fail closed with a clear error + runbook instead of a silent cascade
